---
title: "New features of<br>rtauargus"
subtitle: "a step further in the automation of the analysis"
institute: Insee
author: Clara Baudry
date: today
date-format: "DD/MM/YYYY"
description: |
  template de support de présentation interactif intégrant la charte graphique de l'Insee

slide-tone: false # for blind readers
chalkboard: false # press the B key to toggle chalkboard
jump-to-slide: true #press "g" to jump and the number of slide
mouse-wheel: false
transition: slide
# multiplex: true

embed-resources: false

custom-toc: true

editor:
  render-on-save: true

params:
  annee: "2023"
  codeDepartement: "87"
  codeRegion: "75"
  typeEquipement: "G103"
---

# Recap on the analysis of a publication {#chapters_1 .backgroundTitre_bleu}

## Titre transparent {#section_0_8 .backgroundStandard .titreTransparent .unnumbered data-menu-title="Slide sans titre ni numérotation" visibility="uncounted"}

:::{.enteteAvecTitre}
Recap on the analysis of a publication
:::

![Table protection workflow](img/auto_analysis_workflow.png)

## Importing Eurostat template for EBSBDS[^1] ✍️ {#section_1_1 .backgroundStandard_bleu}

:::{.enteteAvecTitre}
Recap on the analysis of a publication
:::

- ✍️ Manual steps with R
- ⚙️ Package functions

```{r}
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| echo: true

library(dplyr)
library(rtauargus)
```

```{r}
#| echo: true
template_EBSBDS <- read.csv2("./metadata/EBSBDS_ALL_A_FR.csv")
head(template_EBSBDS)
```


[^1]: EBSBDS, European Business Statistics - Buisness Demography Statistics.

## Selecting the columns needed for the analysis ✍️ {#section_1_2 .backgroundStandard_bleu}

:::{.enteteAvecTitre}
Recap on the analysis of a publication
:::

```{r}
#| echo: true
sub_template <- template_EBSBDS %>% 
  filter(INDICATOR %in% c("SAL","SAL_DTH")) %>%
  select(TIME_PERIOD,INDICATOR,ACTIVITY,NUMBER_EMPL,LEGAL_FORM)

print(head(sub_template), row.names = FALSE)
```


SAL: number of employees  
SAL_DTH: number of employees in enterprise deaths

## Going from the template to the description of<br>the published tables ⚙️ {#section_1_3 .backgroundStandard_bleu}

:::{.enteteAvecTitre}
Recap on the analysis of a publication
:::

```{r}
#| echo: true
demo_formatted <- format_template(
  data = sub_template,
  indicator_column = "INDICATOR",
  spanning_var_tot = list(
    ACTIVITY = "BTSXO_S94",
    NUMBER_EMPL = "_T",
    LEGAL_FORM = "_T"),
  field_columns = c("TIME_PERIOD")
)

demo_formatted$metadata
```

The 22 572 rows of the template actually represented 6 tables to publish.

## Adding the hierarchies for field and indicator ✍️ {#section_1_4 .backgroundStandard_bleu}

:::{.enteteAvecTitre}
Recap on the analysis of a publication
:::


```{r}
#| echo: true

metadata_demo <- demo_formatted$metadata %>% 
  mutate(hrc_field = NA, hrc_indicator = NA)

head(metadata_demo)
```

## Analysing the links between tables ⚙️ {#section_1_5 .backgroundStandard_bleu}

:::{.enteteAvecTitre}
Recap on the analysis of a publication
:::


```{r}
#| echo: true

tables_to_protect <- analyse_metadata(
  df_metadata = metadata_demo,
  verbose = FALSE
)

tables_to_protect
```

The analysis shows that 6 tables need to be protected in 3 different clusters.

          
# New features of rtauargus {#chapters_2 .backgroundTitre_violet}

## New features {#section_2_1 .backgroundStandard_bleu}

:::{.enteteAvecTitre}
New features of rtauargus
:::

New parameter added to `analyse_metadata()` allowing the user to specify links between response variables (version [1.3.2](https://github.com/InseeFrLab/rtauargus/releases/tag/v-1.3.2)).

Fix bug due to encoding, french months with accents such as "Décembre" or "Février" (version [1.3.3](https://github.com/InseeFrLab/rtauargus/releases/tag/v-1.3.3)).


## Example of links between response variables {#section_2_2 .backgroundStandard_bleu}

:::{.enteteAvecTitre}
New features of rtauargus
:::


::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="33%"}
**Table 1. Active enterprises**

+------------+-----+-----+-------+
|            | 0–9 | 10+ | Total |
+============+=====+=====+=======+
| **North**  | 120 | 30  | 150   |
+------------+-----+-----+-------+
| **South**  | 100 | 23  | 123   |
+------------+-----+-----+-------+
| **Total**  | 220 | 53  | 273   |
+------------+-----+-----+-------+

:::
::: {.column width="33%"}
**Table 2. Enterprise births**

|        | 0–9 | 10+ | Total |
|--------|-----|-----|-------|
| **North** | [20]{.fragment .highlight-blue fragment-index=1} | [5]{.fragment .highlight-blue fragment-index=1} | 25 |
| **South** | [18]{.fragment .highlight-blue fragment-index=1} | [2]{.fragment .highlight-red fragment-index=1} | 20 |
| **Total** | 38 | 7 | 45 |

:::
::: {.column width="33%"}
**Table 3. Surviving enterprises**

|        | 0–9 | 10+ | Total |
|--------|-----|-----|-------|
| **North** | [100]{.fragment .highlight-blue fragment-index=2} | [25]{.fragment .highlight-blue fragment-index=2} | 125 |
| **South** | [82]{.fragment .highlight-blue fragment-index=2} | [21]{.fragment .highlight-blue fragment-index=2} | 103 |
| **Total** | 182 | 46 | 228 |

:::
:::
*Note: Active enterprises = Enterprise births + Surviving enterprises.*


## Protecting tables with links between<br>response variables {#section_2_3 .backgroundStandard_bleu}

:::{.enteteAvecTitre}
New features of rtauargus
:::

|  GEO  | SIZE CLASS |   ACTIVE  | FREQ |
|:-----:|:----------:|:---------:|:----:|
| North |     0-9    |   birth   | 20   |
| North |     10+    |   birth   | 5    |
| North |     0-9    | surviving | 100  |
| North |     10+    | surviving | 25   |
| North |     0-9    |   active  | 120  |
| North |     10+    |   active  | 30   |
| South |     0-9    |   birth   | 18   |
| South |     10+    |   birth   | 2    |
|  ...  |     ...    |    ...    |      |
| Total |    Total   |   active  | 275  |


# Use case: Annual survey on industrial energy consumption {#chapters_3 .backgroundTitre_jaune}

## From crossings to published tables {#section_3_1 .backgroundStandard_bleu}

:::{.enteteAvecTitre}
Annual survey on industrial energy consumption
:::

![Extract of the French template, one row equals one cell (same as Eurostat template).](./img/extrait_melodi_eacei.png){width=100}

## Equations of response variable {#section_3_2 .backgroundStandard_bleu}

:::{.enteteAvecTitre}
Annual survey on industrial energy consumption
:::

+------------+--------------------------------------+------+
| eq_name    | eq_indicator                         | unit |
+============+======================================+======+
| eq_1       | CONSBRT = BR_CSTP + CAR_CSTP + ...   | TOE  |
+------------+--------------------------------------+------+
| eq_2       | BR_CSTP = BR_USQ1 + BR_USQ2 + ...    | TOE  |
+------------+--------------------------------------+------+
| eq_6       | FACTURE = BIOR_VALA + BR_VALA + ...  | EUR  |
+------------+--------------------------------------+------+
| eq_17      | BR_PROD = BR_THER + BR_NTHE          | GWH  |
+------------+--------------------------------------+------+

## Links between indicators {#section_3_3 .backgroundStandard_bleu}

:::{.enteteAvecTitre}
Annual survey on industrial energy consumption
:::


:::{style="display: flex; justify-content: space-between; align-items: flex-end;"}
![](./img/graphe_analyse_equations_eacei_rogne.png){style="margin-top: -50px !important;"}

[Open interactive graph](https://inseefrlab.github.io/automatic_analysis_poster_NTTS2025/graph_eq_EACEI.html){.button}
:::

## Result {#section_3_4 .backgroundStandard_bleu}

:::{.enteteAvecTitre}
Annual survey on industrial energy consumption
:::

| cluster | table | field | indicator | span_1 | span_2 | hrc_1 | hrc_2 |
|---------|-------|-------|-----------|--------|--------|-------|-------|
| alpha | T1 | 2023 | EUR | GEO | eq6 | hrc_geo13 | hrc_eq6 |
| beta | T2 | 2023 | TOE | GEO | eq1_eq2_... | hrc_geo13 | hrc_eq1_eq2_... |
| beta | T3 | 2023 | TOE | GEO | eq75 | hrc_geo13 | hrc_eq75 |
| ... | ... | ... | ... | ... | ... | ... | ... |

- 72 tables to publish by region (218 in the full publication)
- 9 tables to protect
- 6 clusters


## {#pageDeFin data-menu-title="Page finale" .unnumbered .backgroundPageFinale}

:::: {.columns .coordonneesFinales}

::: {.column .gauche .coordonnees width=30%}
- Clara Baudry
- Methodologist
- [DMGC]{.ligneBlancheApres}
- [DMCSI]{.ligneBlancheApres}
- clara.baudry@insee.fr
- +33 (0)1 87 69 55 15 
:::

::: {.column .coordonnees .milieu width=30%}

:::


::: {.column .coordonnees  .droite width=30%}

:::

::::
